tldr: Both CockroachDB and Xata speak Postgres, so the data and SQL itself port relatively well — the real work is reworking distributed-SQL features (multi-region, serializable txns) that Xata may not match one-for-one.
why_switch: Want a fully managed serverless DBaaS rather than running CockroachDB clusters or Serverless tiers
Looking for simpler, transparent freemium pricing for smaller workloads
Team values Xata's branching, search, and developer-friendly SDK/CLI
Don't need CockroachDB's multi-region distributed write topology
Prefer a Postgres-compatible product without managing CRDB-specific operational concerns
what_to_export: Schema (DDL) for all databases and tables
Table data via pg_dump / SQL dump or CSV
Backup files and any change data capture sinks
export_method: CockroachDB is PostgreSQL-wire-compatible and supports pg_dump-compatible exports as well as its own BACKUP / EXPORT statements. For a portable migration, dumping schema and data to SQL or CSV is usually the cleanest path. Confirm the current recommended export commands in the official CockroachDB docs.
import_method: In Xata, recreate the schema using their CLI / SDK or by running DDL, then load CSV or SQL data via Xata's supported import tooling. Because Xata is Postgres-backed, much of the SQL transfers directly. Verify the current import paths and any size limits in Xata's official documentation.

concept_mapping.note: Schema usually transfers with minor type adjustments
Standard Postgres index types should map directly
Confirm Xata's regional options; CRDB-style geo-partitioning is not a given
Verify isolation levels available in Xata before relying on serializable semantics
Different backup model — check Xata docs for retention and restore
Replace CRDB-only syntax with standard Postgres

gotchas: CRDB-specific SQL (e.g. AS OF SYSTEM TIME, locality clauses) needs to be removed or rewritten
Multi-region writes are a CRDB strength; do not assume Xata offers the same topology — check current docs
Isolation level differences can change app behavior under contention
Validate current Xata storage / row / connection limits against your CRDB workload
Pricing shape changes from CRDB Serverless RU-style to Xata plan limits — rebaseline cost
Tooling around schema migrations may differ; pick a migration tool that targets vanilla Postgres

email.subject: Migrating from CockroachDB to Xata
email.body:
Hi team,

We are planning to move our database off CockroachDB onto Xata to simplify operations and pricing. Both speak Postgres, so the schema and most queries should port without major rewrites.

The real work is around CRDB-specific features: multi-region locality, serializable semantics, and any custom SQL. Near-term we will dump schema and data, identify CRDB-only syntax, and prototype one service on Xata before committing. Please flag any queries that depend on CRDB-specific behavior.

Thanks!
